mnemonics.title: Tree Property Check
mnemonics.content: To verify a graph is a TREE: Count vertices (n), edges should be n-1, must be connected, and must have ZERO cycles. Think 'T-R-E-E': Totally Ringless, Edges Equal n-1.

mnemonics.title: Rooted Tree Root Rule
mnemonics.content: In a ROOTED tree, one node is special—pick any node as root and designate parent/child relationships flowing away from it. The ROOT has no parent, all others have exactly one parent.

mnemonics.title: Path Length Count
mnemonics.content: Path LENGTH = number of EDGES (not vertices). Shortest path from node A to B? Count the bridges you cross! From root to leaf with 3 edges = path length 3.

mnemonics.title: Leaf Node Reminder
mnemonics.content: LEAF nodes have NO children. Remember: Leaves are 'lazy'—they don't produce any branches! In a rooted tree, if node has degree 1 (and isn't root), it's a leaf.

mnemonics.title: Height vs Depth
mnemonics.content: HEIGHT of node = longest path from that node DOWN to a leaf. DEPTH of node = path from ROOT UP to that node. Root: depth=0, height=max. Think 'Height goes Down, Depth goes Up'.

acronyms.usage: For two nodes in a rooted tree, the LCA is the deepest node that is an ancestor of both. Used in path calculations and tree algorithms.
Level-by-level traversal. Useful for finding shortest path in unweighted trees—explores all nodes at distance d before d+1.
Explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking. Useful for path finding and tree traversals.

visualTips.visualization: Draw rooted trees with ROOT at TOP, branches flowing DOWNWARD. Like an upside-down family tree or organizational chart. Each edge connects parent above to child below.
Highlight the path between two nodes by tracing a continuous line through ancestors/descendants. Count each edge segment—this is your path length.
Mark leaf nodes with circles at branch tips (no lines extending further down). In binary trees, draw leaves as endpoints with no children.
For each node, trace ALL downward paths to leaves, count edges in longest one—that's height. Root's height = tree height.

analogies.visualization: ROOT = oldest ancestor. PARENT = direct predecessor. CHILD = direct descendant. SIBLINGS = same parent. Great-grandparent = ancestor 3 levels up. Path length = generations between two family members.

CEO = ROOT (no boss above). Managers = internal nodes with both boss and subordinates. Entry-level employees = LEAVES (no one reports to them). Levels = depth from CEO. Path from CEO to any employee = number of management layers between them.

ROOT = trunk base. BRANCHES = internal nodes. LEAVES = leaf nodes at branch tips. Path from root to leaf = trunk + branches + leaf stem. Height = ground to highest leaf tip. Depth = how far a branch is from the trunk.
